exercises.prompt: List every printing of the card 'Red-Eyes Black Dragon'. Join cards to card_sets and return the card name, set name, set code, and rarity of each printing (one row per printing), using the columns' own names.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name, cs.set_name, cs.set_code, cs.rarity FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE c.name = 'Red-Eyes Black Dragon'
hints: Match cards to their printings with JOIN ... ON c.id = cs.card_id, then filter to the single card by name.
FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE c.name = 'Red-Eyes Black Dragon', selecting c.name, cs.set_name, cs.set_code, cs.rarity.

```sql
SELECT c.name, cs.set_name, cs.set_code, cs.rarity FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE c.name = 'Red-Eyes Black Dragon'
```

exercises.prompt: Every card that is Forbidden in the TCG format and belongs to an archetype: return the card name and its archetype. Include only cards that have an archetype (ignore cards whose archetype is NULL), ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name, c.archetype FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.name
hints: Join cards to banlist on c.id = b.card_id, then keep only the TCG rows whose status is Forbidden and whose card has an archetype.
WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL, then ORDER BY c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name, c.archetype FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.name
```

exercises.prompt: For each card in the 'Dark Magician' archetype, how many times has it been printed? Join cards to card_sets and return name and the printing count as printing_count — most-printed first, breaking ties alphabetically by name.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) AS printing_count FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE c.archetype = 'Dark Magician' GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY printing_count DESC, c.name
hints: After joining cards to their printings, group by the card name and COUNT the rows in each group.
GROUP BY c.name with COUNT(*) AS printing_count, then ORDER BY printing_count DESC, c.name.

```sql
SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) AS printing_count FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE c.archetype = 'Dark Magician' GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY printing_count DESC, c.name
```

exercises.prompt: Which cards have ever been printed at 'Starlight Rare' rarity? Return each qualifying card's name once, with no duplicates, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE cs.rarity = 'Starlight Rare' ORDER BY c.name
hints: Join to card_sets and filter on the rarity; because a card can be printed at that rarity more than once, DISTINCT keeps it to one row.
SELECT DISTINCT c.name ... WHERE cs.rarity = 'Starlight Rare' ORDER BY c.name.

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM cards c JOIN card_sets cs ON c.id = cs.card_id WHERE cs.rarity = 'Starlight Rare' ORDER BY c.name
```

exercises.prompt: How many Forbidden-in-TCG cards does each archetype have? Join cards to banlist, keep the TCG Forbidden cards that have an archetype, and return archetype with the count as forbidden_count — most first, breaking ties alphabetically by archetype.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT c.archetype, COUNT(*) AS forbidden_count FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.archetype ORDER BY forbidden_count DESC, c.archetype
hints: Join cards to banlist, filter to TCG Forbidden cards with an archetype, then group by archetype and count.
WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL, GROUP BY c.archetype, ORDER BY forbidden_count DESC, c.archetype.

```sql
SELECT c.archetype, COUNT(*) AS forbidden_count FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' AND c.archetype IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.archetype ORDER BY forbidden_count DESC, c.archetype
```

exercises.prompt: Which sets contain the most Forbidden-in-TCG cards? Join cards, banlist, and card_sets, and for each set count the distinct TCG-Forbidden cards printed in it. Return set_name and the count as forbidden_count for sets containing at least 5 such cards — most first, breaking ties alphabetically by set_name.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT cs.set_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS forbidden_count FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id JOIN card_sets cs ON cs.card_id = c.id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' GROUP BY cs.set_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) >= 5 ORDER BY forbidden_count DESC, cs.set_name
hints: Chain two joins off cards — one to banlist for the Forbidden filter, one to card_sets for the set — then group by set and count distinct cards.
GROUP BY cs.set_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS forbidden_count, HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) >= 5, ORDER BY forbidden_count DESC, cs.set_name.

```sql
SELECT cs.set_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS forbidden_count FROM cards c JOIN banlist b ON c.id = b.card_id JOIN card_sets cs ON cs.card_id = c.id WHERE b.format = 'tcg' AND b.status = 'Forbidden' GROUP BY cs.set_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) >= 5 ORDER BY forbidden_count DESC, cs.set_name
```
